rationale: Round-2 fix re-verified gate-forward; my round-1 APPROVE stands. `mindspec validate plan 109-orchestration-config-substrate` exits 0 (sole issue: advisory decomposition-scope-redundancy R=0.06 < 0.15 — the intentional disjoint cut, unchanged from round 1). The wiring bead (B4) now adds internal/complete/panel_advisory.go{,_test.go} and gains dep on B3. (1) DOMAIN SET: internal/complete/** is uniquely workflow (grep confirms it is claimed only by workflow/OWNERSHIP.yaml, absent from core/execution/context-system), the SAME domain cmd/** already put B4 in — no new domain enters, so the divergence gate's per-bead coverage is unchanged and workflow stays covered by all four cited ADRs (0035/0039 carry core,workflow; 0037/0034 workflow). (2) DOC-SYNC still correct + bead-unique: checkInternalPackages groups source by ATTRIBUTED DOMAIN and requires one domains/<domain> doc per domain, not per package — both workflow source files are satisfied by the single .mindspec/domains/workflow/interfaces.md, still distinct from B3's architecture.md. Test files aren't source (built-in classifier excludes _test.go). (3) CHAIN DEPTH ≤ heuristic: diamond 1→2→4 / 1→3→4 = 3 nodes; MaxChainDepth default 3 warns only on >3; validator fired no chain-depth or parallelism warning (parallelism 1/4 = 0.25 = floor). Named complete-gate surface + seams verified present on disk.
findings: VALIDATOR (re-run at d53c5939): `mindspec validate plan 109-orchestration-config-substrate` exits 0; only WARN is decomposition-scope-redundancy R=0.06 (<0.15), byte-identical severity to round 1 — advisory, non-blocking, plan owns it. No decomposition-chain-depth and no decomposition-parallelism WARN despite the new diamond, confirming the graph metrics stay under threshold after adding the B3→B4 edge.

DOMAIN SET UNCHANGED BY THE COMPLETE TOUCH: internal/complete/** appears in workflow/OWNERSHIP.yaml ONLY (verified: grep -l finds just workflow; execution/context-system carry neither internal/complete nor cmd/). B4 already sat in workflow via cmd/** (cmd/mindspec/config.go), so the added internal/complete/panel_advisory.go attributes to the same workflow domain — the divergence gate sees no new impacted domain and workflow remains covered by all four cited ADRs at complete-time. B2 (internal/config→core) is still the only core-touching bead; core coverage by 0035+0039 is untouched.

DOC-SYNC TARGET CORRECT + BEAD-UNIQUE: docsync.checkInternalPackages attributes each source file to a domain and, per domain with source, requires a hasArtifactPrefix(doc,"domains/<domain>") hit — this is PER-DOMAIN, not per-package. B4's two workflow source files (cmd/mindspec/config.go + internal/complete/panel_advisory.go) are both satisfied by the single carried .mindspec/domains/workflow/interfaces.md. That doc is distinct from B3's .mindspec/domains/workflow/architecture.md, so the two beads stay bead-unique. `_test.go` files are not source (isSourceFile excludes them), so config_test.go / panel_advisory_test.go impose no extra doc requirement.

cmd-docs WARN UNCHANGED / NON-BLOCKING: checkCmdChanges emits an advisory `cmd-docs` Warn because interfaces.md is a domain doc, not in its operator-doc accept set (CLAUDE.md/CONVENTIONS.md/user-docs/USAGE). This warn already applied to B4 in round 1 (cmd/mindspec touch) and is unchanged by the internal/complete addition — advisory only (AddWarning), never blocks the complete gate. Not a defect and not new.

CHAIN DEPTH ≤ HEURISTIC: plan.go:1081 warns only when chainDepth > cfg.MaxChainDepth (default 3, config.go:87). The diamond's longest path is 1→2→4 and 1→3→4 = 3 nodes; the B3→B4 edge adds breadth, not depth, so depth stays 3 (== threshold, not over). parallelism = zeroInbound(1: only B1)/4 = 0.25 == MinParallelism floor (warns only on <0.25). Empirically confirmed: validator fired neither decomposition warning.

SURFACE + SEAMS REAL (not phantom): internal/complete/panel_advisory.go and panel_advisory_test.go exist on disk; the named seams panelScanFn (=panel.Scan), panelTallyFn (=panel.Tally), panelAdvisoryOut (io.Writer), and panel.ForBead are already present and used there, and complete.go:304 wires advisoryOut=panelAdvisoryOut. So B4's caller-side ReviewerCountNote wiring (panelReg.Panel.ExpectedReviewers vs cfg.PanelExpectedReviewers(), advisory-only, PanelGateDecision untouched) targets a live surface with the exact test seams the plan names — the key_file_paths and doc-sync reasoning point at real code.
